As a result, Rudolph was slammed with felony counts of vehicle fleeing eluding an officer, second degree recklessly endangering safety, OWI causing injury, prohibited alcohol content causing injury, misdemeanor counts of operating while intoxicated, third offense, resisting obstructing an officer, and operating with prohibited alcohol content, third offense.
He received over $1,300 in traffic citations and was subsequently given a $5,000 cash bond.

As of February 2024, Rudolph was found guilty of only two felony charges. Two counts were dismissed on the prosecutor's motion, and the remaining were dismissed but read in. In the end, he was sentenced to 18 months of initial confinement, followed by two years of extended supervision.
